footnotes.text: Weak in faith: those who had an erroneous conscience concerning the implications of the Christian teaching, especially concerning certain foods. The strong in faith are those who are better instructed and understand that no food is of itself unclean and forbidden. St. Paul in this chapter exhorts the strong to bear with and refrain from scandalizing the weak, and the weak to refrain from condemning the strong. Judgment is to be left to our Lord, before whose tribunal all men must appear.
The sense seems to be: "A man who, by eating, gives scandal." Others understand: "Who eats under the influence of scandal."
Faith: here means conscience enlightened by faith.
